data.title: Silver Bow Mining, Montana Silver-Zinc-Gold Explorer, Files for NYSE American IPO
data.description:
Silver Bow Mining filed an S-1 to list its common shares on the NYSE American. The exploration-stage company focuses on silver, zinc, gold, lead, and copper in Montana’s historic Butte Mining District. Cantor Fitzgerald & Co. and Research Capital USA Inc. are joint bookrunners, and proceeds will fund exploration and general corporate purposes.

Business Description

Silver Bow Mining is a minerals exploration company focused on silver, zinc, gold, lead, and copper targets in Silver Bow County, Montana. Its flagship Rainbow Block covers approximately 878 acres within the historic Butte Mining District and benefits from extensive historical drilling, sampling, and underground workings. The company controls about 3,347 acres of patented mineral claims across the district, with additional blocks at Marget Ann, Goldsmith, Travona, and Emma.

An independent S-K 1300 Technical Report established an initial Inferred Mineral Resource at Rainbow Block of 11.48 million tons grading 14.8 oz/ton Ag-Eq (507.4 g/t), containing 170.0 million ounces Ag-Eq, based on silver, gold, lead and zinc. The project currently has no measured or indicated resources or reserves, and Silver Bow plans to advance exploration through a surface drilling program and underground rehabilitation of the Chief Joseph decline. The company intends to use IPO proceeds to fund drilling, underground access, and related studies to advance Rainbow Block toward feasibility.

Market Overview

  • Total addressable market: Global demand for silver, zinc, gold, lead, and copper spans industrial, technology, and investment uses.
  • Market growth: Rising electrification and renewable energy adoption support demand for silver (solar, electronics) and zinc (galvanized steel).
  • Market position: Early-stage explorer in the Butte district with an initial inferred resource at the Rainbow Block.
  • Key competitors: North American precious and base metals explorers and producers operating in established mining jurisdictions.
  • Industry trends: Focus on critical minerals security in the U.S., modernization of legacy districts, and increased use of data-driven exploration.

Operational Metrics

  • Customers: Not applicable (exploration-stage; no commercial production)
  • Locations: Headquarters in Butte, Montana; projects across the Butte Mining District
  • Geographic presence: Silver Bow County, Montana, USA
  • Partnerships: Exploration license and permits with Montana DEQ; service providers include geological and assay labs
  • Orders/GMV: Not applicable
  • Other key metrics: ~3,347 acres of patented mineral claims across district; Rainbow Block ~878 acres (mineral rights) with ~215 acres of surface owned
  • Other key metrics: Initial Inferred Resource: 11.48M tons at 14.8 oz/ton Ag-Eq (170.0 Moz Ag-Eq); 42 distinct veins identified to date
  • Other key metrics: Exploration License #000857 allows up to 10 drill pads, 35 diamond drill holes (to ~1,500 ft), and up to 10,000 tons of material for testing
  • Other key metrics: Planned 2026 program includes ~35,000 ft of surface drilling and rehabilitation of the Chief Joseph decline for underground drilling
  • Other key metrics: Reclamation bond of $225,788 posted; estimated exploration-stage reclamation liability ~$214,000
  • Other key metrics: Workforce includes ~12 employees and 4 contractors (as of Dec. 15, 2025)
  • Other key metrics: No water rights currently at Rainbow Block; exploration water available for purchase from local utility

Financials Highlights

  • Revenue (current): None (exploration-stage; no revenue)
  • Net income: Net loss of $9,124,931 for the nine months ended September 30, 2025; net loss of $4,808,639 in 2024

Management

  • C. Travis Naugle, Chief Executive Officer and Chairman - Mining executive and licensed Professional Engineer with 25+ years in copper, zinc, silver, and gold projects; former CEO of Lion Copper and Gold.
  • Wade Black, Chief Financial Officer - 30+ years in financial services and brokerage operations; previously COO at Scarsdale Equities and Principal at R.F. Lafferty & Co.
  • Peter Burroughs, Chief Accounting Officer - 30+ years in corporate finance and accounting across mining and industrials; former VP Finance at Martin Marietta and finance roles at Caterpillar.
  • Phillip Nickerson, Vice President of Exploration - Economic geologist with U.S. base and precious metals experience; formerly with EMX Royalty and Rio Tinto; Ph.D. in Geoscience.
  • Quinton Hennigh, Director - Economic geologist with 25+ years at Homestake, Newcrest, and Newmont; founder and leader at several exploration companies.
  • David McMullin, Director - Senior executive with 30+ years across mining, banking, and operations; leadership roles in multiple resource companies.
  • Steve Durbin, Director - Investor and private equity executive; founder of Quail Bend Capital Partners with cross-industry investment experience.
  • Andy Holloway, Director - Metallurgical engineer with 35+ years in plant design and project management across base and precious metals globally.

IPO Structure

  • Issuer: SILVER BOW MINING CORP.
  • Filing date: January 23, 2026
  • Exchange: NYSE American
  • Lead underwriters: Cantor Fitzgerald & Co.; Research Capital USA Inc.
  • Use of proceeds: Fund ongoing mining and exploration activities, and for working capital and general corporate purposes.

Original SEC Filing: SILVER BOW MINING CORP. - S-1 - Jan. 23, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: DEFSEC Technologies Strengthens Board with New Appointment and Provides Shareholder Meeting Update
data.description:
Ottawa, Ontario--(Newsfile Corp. - January 23, 2026) - DEFSEC Technologies Inc. (TSXV: DFSC.WT.U) ("DEFSEC" or the "Company") is pleased to announce that it has augmented its board team with a strong new appointment.

Niel Marotta has been appointed as a member of the board, effective January 23, 2026. He brings extensive capital markets experience with emerging companies, and currently serves as Chief Executive Officer of Sun Summit Minerals Corp. . Niel is an accomplished executive with an exceptional track record of leadership, investment expertise, and entrepreneurial success. Prior to joining Sun Summit Minerals, he was a top-performing fund manager at Fidelity Management and Research Company (FMR Co.) in Boston, where he managed over $1 billion across multiple industries, including the Fidelity Select Gold Portfolio, a precious metals equity focussed fund. Mr. Marotta also served as Vice President of Orezone Resources, playing a key role in its $350 million acquisition by Iamgold in 2009. He has worked as an investment banker and entrepreneur and has been involved with financings and M&A transactions exceeding $1 billion. He was also the co-founder and CEO of Indiva Limited. Mr. Marotta holds a Bachelor of Commerce with Distinction from McGill University.

"We're delighted to welcome Niel to the DEFSEC team," said Sean Homuth, President and CEO. "Fiscal 2025 was a year of very strong growth in revenue momentum and we anticipate continued growth through Fiscal 2026, making this the right time to add exceptional talent and capacity for the many opportunities ahead."

Amended Management Information Circular

The Company previously filed a management information circular dated January 5, 2026 (the "Original Circular") and form of proxy (the "Original Proxy") in connection with its annual and special meeting of shareholders (the "Meeting") to be held on February 12, 2026. As a result of the recent addition of Mr. Marotta to the board of directors as noted above, the Company will be filing an amended and restated notice of the annual and special meeting of shareholders, to be held on February 19, 2026, and a related amended and restated management information circular (the "Amended Circular") and form of proxy (the "Amended Proxy").

The Amended Circular replaces and supersedes the Original Circular, to reflect: (i) the increase in the number of directors to be fixed from six (6) to seven (7); (ii) the addition of Niel Marotta as a director nominee; (iii) the treatment of completed and received proxies prior to the date hereof; and (iv) the change of the Meeting date to February 19, 2026. The Amended Circular is unchanged in all other respects, including the original record date of January 2, 2026.

Original Proxies provided in connection with the Original Circular that have already been completed and returned will remain valid. Any Original Proxy received from a shareholder shall be used, pursuant to the discretionary authority conferred by the form of proxy, to vote: (i) "FOR" the number of directors of the Company to be set at seven (7); and (ii) "FOR" the election of Niel Marotta as a director of the Company. Shareholders do not need to complete the Amended Proxy unless they wish to vote "AGAINST" or "WITHHOLD", as applicable, on either of these resolutions. If shareholders wish to vote "AGAINST" or "WITHHOLD", as applicable, on either of these resolutions, they may do so by filing the Amended Proxy as discussed and enclosed in the Amended Circular. Any completed and returned Amended Proxy shall automatically supersede and revoke the Original Proxy.

For further information, please contact:

Jennifer Welsh, Chief Financial Officer and Chief Compliance Officer

welsh@defsectec.com

Sean Homuth, President and Chief Executive Officer

homuth@defsectec.com

Jason Frame, Investor Relations

+1 (587) 225-2599

frame@defsectec.com

About DEFSEC

DEFSEC (TSXV: DFSC.WT.U) (NASDAQ: DFSCSW) (FSE: 62UA) develops and commercializes breakthrough next-generation tactical systems for military and security forces. The company's current portfolio of offerings includes digitization of tactical forces for real-time shared situational awareness and targeting information from any source (including drones) streamed directly to users' smart devices and weapons. Other DEFSEC products include countermeasures against threats such as electronic detection, lasers and drones. These systems can operate stand-alone or integrate seamlessly with OEM products and battlefield management systems, and all come integrated with TAK. The company also has a new proprietary non-lethal product line branded PARA SHOTTM with applications across all segments of the non-lethal market, including law enforcement. The Company is headquartered in Ottawa, Canada, with a representative office in London, UK.

For more information, please visit https://www.defsectec.com

Forward-Looking Statements

This news release contains "forward-looking statements" and "forward-looking information" within the meaning of Canadian and United States securities laws (collectively, "forward-looking statements"), which may be identified by the use of terms and phrases such as "may", "would", "should", "could", "expect", "intend", "estimate", "anticipate", "plan", "foresee", "believe", or "continue", the negative of these terms and similar terminology, including references to assumptions, although not all forward-looking statements contain these terms and phrases. Forward-looking statements are provided for the purpose of assisting the reader in understanding us, our business, operations, prospects and risks at a point in time in the context of historical and possible future developments and therefore the reader is cautioned that such information may not be appropriate for other purposes. Such forward-looking statements are based on the current expectations of DEFSEC's management and are based on assumptions and subject to risks and uncertainties. Forward-looking statements included in this include, but are not limited to: management's belief of sufficiency of available financial resources to support forecasted activities in 2026 based on cash on hand, anticipated revenue streams and planned expenditures in the fiscal year, subject to execution of the Company's operating plan and other factors described in its public filings; and forecasted revenue growth in future financial periods of the Company;

Although DEFSEC's management believes that the assumptions underlying such forward-looking statements are reasonable, they may prove to be incorrect. The forward-looking statements discussed in this news release may not occur by certain specified dates or at all and could differ materially as a result of known and unknown risk factors and uncertainties affecting DEFSEC, including DEFSEC's inability to execute on its current operating plan and/or forecasted activities in upcoming financial periods of the Company; general economic and stock market conditions; a stagnation or decrease in North American defense and public safety spending, adverse industry events; future legislative and regulatory developments in Canada, the United States and elsewhere; the inability of DEFSEC to implement and execute its business strategies; risks and uncertainties detailed from time to time in DEFSEC's filings with the Canadian Security Administrators and the United States Securities and Exchange Commission; and many other factors beyond the control of DEFSEC. Although DEFSEC has attempted to identify important factors that could cause actual actions, events or results to differ materially from those described in forward-looking statements, there may be other factors that cause actions, events or results to differ from those anticipated, estimated or intended. Except as required by applicable securities laws, forward-looking statements speak only as of the date on which they are made and DEFSEC undertakes no obligation to publicly update or revise any forward-looking statements, whether as a result of new information, future events or otherwise.

Neither the TSX Venture Exchange nor its respective Regulation Services Provider (as that term is defined in the policies of the TSX Venture Exchange) accepts responsibility for the adequacy or accuracy of this news release.

data.title: Early Warning Press Release – Acquisitions by Pierre Lassonde in Fuerte Metals
data.description:
TORONTO, ON / ACCESS Newswire / January 23, 2026 / This press release is being disseminated pursuant to National Instrument 62-103 The Early Warning System and Related Take-Over Bid and Insider Reporting Issues ("National Instrument 62-103") by Mr. Pierre Lassonde ("Mr. Lassonde") in respect of his ownership position in Fuerte Metals Corporation ("Fuerte" or the "Company") (OTCQB:FUEMF).

Mr. Lassonde reports that on October 9, 2025, he acquired 4,900,000 subscription receipts (the "Subscription Receipts") issued by 1555489 B.C. Ltd., a wholly-owned subsidiary of Fuerte Metals Corporation ("Fuerte"), pursuant to a brokered private placement, at a price of C$1.65 per Subscription Receipt for total consideration of C$8,085,000 (the "Private Placement").

On October 17, 2025, upon satisfaction of the escrow release conditions set out in the subscription receipt indenture (the "Escrow Release Conditions"), each Subscription Receipt automatically converted into one unit of Fuerte (a "Unit"). Each Unit was comprised of one common share in the capital of Fuerte (a "Common Share") and one Common Share purchase warrant (a "Warrant"). Each Warrant entitles the holder to acquire one Common Share at a price of C$2.50 for a period of five years from the date of issue. One of the Escrow Release Conditions was the completion of the acquisition of certain properties by Fuerte, and in connection with such acquisition, Fuerte issued, as partial consideration therefor, 34,848,485 Common Shares (or securities convertible into Common Shares) (the "Transaction").

Mr. Lassonde further reports that on December 18, 2025, he acquired an additional 3,054,607 Common Shares comprised of 3,015,620 Common Shares acquired privately at a price of $4.35 per Common Share, and 38,987 Common Shares acquired in the public market at a price of $4.759 per Common Share (the "December Acquisitions").

Prior to the Private Placement, Mr. Lassonde owned 7,145,393 Common Shares, representing approximately 11.65% of the issued and outstanding Common Shares on a non-diluted basis. As a result of the Transaction and the December Acquisitions, Mr. Lassonde now directly owns 15,100,000 Common Shares and 4,900,000 Warrants, representing approximately 11.9% of the issued and outstanding Common Shares on a non-diluted basis and approximately 15.76% of the Common Shares on a partially-diluted basis (assuming exercise of the Warrants held by Mr. Lassonde).

The investments in Fuerte are consistent with Mr. Lassonde's historical practice of holding the Shares for investment purposes. Mr. Lassonde does not currently intend to acquire or dispose of any additional securities of the Company. Nonetheless, Mr. Lassonde may, depending on price, market conditions or other conditions or factors he considers relevant from time to time, increase or decrease his beneficial ownership, control or direction over Common Shares or other securities of the Company through market transactions, private agreements or otherwise.

Further to the requirements of National Instrument 62-103, Mr. Lassonde will file an early warning report in accordance with applicable securities laws. A copy of Mr. Lassonde's related early warning report will be filed with the applicable securities commissions and will be made available under Fuerte's SEDAR+ profile at www.sedarplus.ca.

For further information or a copy of Mr. Lassonde's early warning report, please contact

Pierre Lassonde

45 Hazelton Ave., Suite A

Toronto, ON, M5R 2E3

(416) 444-3906

SOURCE: Mr. Pierre Lassonde

View the original press release on ACCESS Newswire

data.title: Sarepta Therapeutics Shares Rise on Planned Readout of Phase 3 Data
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall

Shares of Sarepta Therapeutics gained after the company said it would present three-year, topline functional results from its Phase 3 study of a treatment for Duchenne muscular dystrophy.

The stock rose 9.9% to $23.23 in post-market trading Friday, after closing the market session 3% lower at $21.13. Share have plunged 82% in the past 12 months.

Sarepta will hold a call Monday morning at 8:30 a.m. ET to present results from Part 1 of the study, known as EMBARK, of Elevidys.

The test involves ambulatory patients with the genetic muscular degeneration disease between the ages four and seven.

Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com

data.title: Lawsuit challenges Trump ending Ethiopians' temporary legal status
data.description:
Key points:
  • Trump administration accused of unconstitutional animus
  • Biden administration extended temporary protected status due to ongoing conflicts

By Nate Raymond

Immigrant rights advocates have filed a lawsuit seeking to block U.S. President Donald Trump's administration from ending temporary protections from deportation that had been granted to thousands of Ethiopians living in the United States.

Three Ethiopian nationals, along with the non-profit African Communities Together, allege in a lawsuit filed in Boston federal court on Thursday that the U.S. Department of Homeland Security is unlawfully putting over 5,000 people at risk of losing their temporary protected status after February 13.

It marked the latest in a series of lawsuits challenging the Trump administration's efforts to curtail protections from deportation extended to citizens of numerous countries through grants of Temporary Protected Status, or TPS.

Under federal law, TPS is available to people whose home countries have experienced natural disasters, armed conflicts or other extraordinary events. It provides eligible migrants with work authorization and temporary protection from deportation.

LAWSUIT ALLEGES ANIMUS AGAINST NON-WHITES

The lawsuit says that while Ethiopia remains a country in a humanitarian crisis, Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem unlawfully terminated the Ethiopians' legal status with just 60 days' notice based on an unconstitutional animus against non-white immigrants. Ethiopia's population is predominantly Black.

"The administration's review of Ethiopia’s TPS designation resulting in the termination decision was motivated wrongly by politics and racism and ignored the rule of law, including the requirement to consider objective evidence of unsafe conditions in Ethiopia," Amaha Kassa, executive director of African Communities Together, said in a statement.

Homeland Security Department spokesperson Tricia McLaughlin, in a statement said TPS "was never intended to be a de facto amnesty program, yet that's how previous administrations have used it for decades."

Numerous lawsuits have been filed challenging the administration's moves to end similar protections for nationals from countries, including Syria, Venezuela, Haiti, Nicaragua and South Sudan, leading to court rulings that have at times slowed or halted the Trump administration's efforts to strip migrants of their legal status.

The latest lawsuit was filed after DHS said on December 12 it was ending TPS for Ethiopia, saying conditions in the African nation no longer posed a serious threat to people returning safely to it and that TPS was "never meant to be a ticket to permanent residency."

Former Democratic President Joe Biden's administration granted Ethiopians already in the United States that status beginning in 2022, citing the need to protect citizens fleeing armed conflict in their home nation.

The Ethiopian military and allies, including troops from neighboring Eritrea, had been battling forces from the northern region of Tigray in a conflict that killed thousands and displaced millions of people.

While that civil war formally ended later in 2022, the Biden administration extended TPS for Ethiopians in mid-2024, citing armed conflict in another region, Amhara, which is ongoing.

data.title: First Financial Bankshares Announces Fourth Quarter and Year-End 2025 Earnings
data.description:
First Financial Bankshares, Inc., headquartered in Abilene, Texas, has released its financial results for the fourth quarter and the year ended December 31, 2025. The company reported significant growth in earnings and other key financial metrics, reflecting a strong performance in the financial year.

Financial Highlights

For the fourth quarter of 2025, First Financial Bankshares reported earnings of $73.31 million, a notable increase from $52.27 million in the third quarter of 2025 and $62.32 million in the same quarter of the previous year. Diluted earnings per share (EPS) for the fourth quarter were $0.51, compared to $0.36 in the third quarter and $0.43 in the fourth quarter of 2024.

For the full year 2025, net income was $253.58 million, or $1.77 per diluted share, up from $223.51 million, or $1.56 per diluted share, in 2024. This represents an increase of $30.07 million, or 13.45 percent.

Net interest income for the fourth quarter was $131.37 million, compared to $127.00 million in the third quarter and $116.12 million in the fourth quarter of 2024. The net interest margin on a tax-equivalent basis was 3.81 percent, slightly up from 3.80 percent in the third quarter and 3.67 percent in the fourth quarter of 2024.

Business and Operational Highlights

The company experienced strong deposit growth during the quarter, which is expected to support loan and securities growth in 2026. Average interest-earning assets were $14.08 billion for the fourth quarter, up from $12.86 billion in the same quarter of the previous year.

Noninterest income for the fourth quarter was $33.35 million, up from $30.98 million in the fourth quarter of 2024. Mortgage income increased to $4.22 million due to higher loan origination volumes and pricing margins. Trust fee income also rose to $13.51 million, driven by growth in the market value of assets under management.

Strategic Initiatives and Corporate Developments

First Financial Bankshares reported a credit provision reversal of $2.49 million for the fourth quarter, compared to a provision for credit losses of $24.44 million in the third quarter and $1.00 million in the fourth quarter of 2024. The allowance for credit losses totaled $105.54 million, or 1.29 percent of total loans, at the end of 2025.

Shareholders' equity increased to $1.92 billion as of December 31, 2025, from $1.61 billion at the end of 2024, driven by growth in retained earnings and improvements in Other Comprehensive Income (OCI) due to lower unrealized losses in the bond portfolio.

Management's Perspective

F. Scott Dueser, Chairman and CEO of First Financial Bankshares, expressed satisfaction with the company's performance, highlighting the record quarterly net income and strong deposit growth. He emphasized the company's focus on sound lending growth, managing the changing interest rate environment, and navigating the regulatory landscape.

Future Outlook

Looking ahead to 2026, the company aims to continue its focus on organic balance sheet growth, increasing earning assets, and growing net interest income. The management remains committed to supporting customers, shareholders, and associates while maintaining a strong financial position.

Original SEC Filing: FIRST FINANCIAL BANKSHARES INC [ FFIN ] - 8-K - Jan. 23, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: California AG Sues Trump Administration to Block Oil Pipeline Restart — WSJ
data.description:
By Clara Hudson

California Attorney General Rob Bonta filed a lawsuit against the Trump administration over its move to restart the Las Flores oil pipelines in the state.

The suit follows a December decision by the Pipeline and Hazardous Materials Safety Administration, an arm of the Transportation Department, to give the green light for a plan by Sable Offshore to restart moving oil through the pipelines. That was after the administration classified the two pipelines — running from Santa Barbara County to Kern County — as "interstate."

The California attorney general and the state fire marshal Friday filed a petition for review in the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Ninth Circuit to challenge the administration's decision, which Bonta described as "an attempt to evade state regulation." The petition says the administration's orders were arbitrary and capricious and violate the Administrative Procedure Act.

However, the administration has said the permits it gave Sable Offshore followed President Trump's executive order declaring a national energy emergency last year.

The pipelines have been dormant for a decade since the 2015 Refugio Beach oil spill, when hundreds of thousands of gallons of oil was released near Santa Barbara. After the oil spill, the state fire marshal was given a role in reviewing any plans to restart the pipelines.

"California has seen firsthand the devastating environmental and public health impacts of coastal oil spills — yet the Trump administration will stop at nothing to evade state regulation which protects against these very disasters," said Bonta.

Environmental groups including the Environmental Defense Center have already made legal bids in an effort to halt the pipeline restart.

"If this project goes forward, we fear another disaster is all but inevitable," the group has said.

Write to Clara Hudson at clara.hudson@wsj.com

data.title: S&P revises Congo's outlook to 'positive' as reforms, mining strength boost economy
data.description:
Global credit ratings agency S&P on Friday revised the Democratic Republic of Congo's outlook to "positive" from "stable," citing expected progress on tax administration and fiscal performance, as well as favourable terms of trade, rising exports and reforms backed by the International Monetary Fund.

The ratings agency said real GDP growth is set to average about 5% through 2028, outpacing most peers as demand for copper and cobalt remains firm. Robust mining output is expected to drive the economy and boost foreign currency reserves.

The S&P's decision comes after a cautiously optimistic assessment of Congo's economy by the IMF in its review of programs with Kinshasa.

The Fund, however, warned of risks, including commodity price fluctuations and the persistence of a conflict with Rwanda-backed rebels in the east of the country, which exerts significant pressure on public finances.

The copper- and cobalt-rich nation this week announced plans to issue a debut Eurobond for $750 million in April, aiming to leverage its fairly low debt levels and the IMF's positive appraisal.

Finance Minister Doudou Fwamba Likunde welcomed S&P's revision, saying it will boost investor confidence ahead of the country's international debt market debut.

The government viewed the decision "as an important recognition of the DRC's economic resilience and the Government's ongoing efforts to strengthen macroeconomic stability," he said in a statement.

The country retained its "B-" long-term and "B" short-term local- and foreign-currency sovereign credit ratings.

data.title: Spring Valley Acquisition III Signs Multiple Material Agreements
data.description:
Spring Valley Acquisition III announced a series of agreements to combine with General Fusion and finance the transaction. The Business Combination Agreement contemplates SVIII's continuation to British Columbia, an amalgamation with a NewCo, and a post-closing name change to General Fusion, with approximately 60,000,000 shares to be issued and up to 12,500,000 earnout shares tied to share price milestones. A Sponsor Letter supports the deal and aligns incentives, while Voting and Support Agreements secure key shareholder approvals. At closing, Registration Rights and Lock-Up Agreements will facilitate orderly liquidity. In parallel, Subscription Agreements provide a PIPE financing of 10,556,367 units at $10.20 per unit, each with a convertible preferred share and warrant.

Agreement 1: Spring Valley Acquisition III Signs Business Combination Agreement With General Fusion

  • Agreement type: Business Combination Agreement
  • Counterparty: General Fusion
  • Signed / Effective: Jan 21 2026 / same
  • Duration / Termination: Until closing
  • Reason: Combine with General Fusion and list on a U.S. exchange

Agreement 2: Spring Valley Acquisition III Signs Sponsor Letter Agreement With Spring Valley Acquisition Sponsor III

  • Agreement type: Sponsor Letter Agreement
  • Counterparty: Spring Valley Acquisition Sponsor III
  • Signed / Effective: Jan 21 2026 / same
  • Duration / Termination: Through closing and post-closing earnout periods
  • Reason: Align sponsor incentives and secure support for the merger

Agreement 3: Spring Valley Acquisition III Signs Voting and Support Agreement With General Fusion Securityholders

  • Agreement type: Voting and Support Agreement
  • Counterparty: General Fusion Securityholders
  • Signed / Effective: Jan 21 2026 / same
  • Duration / Termination: Until closing
  • Reason: Secure shareholder approvals for the transaction

Agreement 4: Spring Valley Acquisition III Signs Registration Rights Agreement With Sponsor and General Fusion Holders

  • Agreement type: Amended and Restated Registration Rights Agreement
  • Counterparty: Sponsor and General Fusion Securityholders
  • Signed / Effective: Jan 21 2026 / same
  • Duration / Termination: Ongoing until resale registration fulfilled
  • Reason: Provide liquidity to key holders post-merger

Agreement 5: Spring Valley Acquisition III Signs Lock-Up Agreements With General Fusion Securityholders

  • Agreement type: Lock-Up Agreements
  • Counterparty: General Fusion Securityholders
  • Signed / Effective: Jan 21 2026 / same
  • Duration / Termination: 180 days after closing
  • Reason: Promote orderly trading following the merger

Agreement 6: Spring Valley Acquisition III Signs Subscription Agreements With Anchor PIPE Investor and Other Accredited Investors

  • Agreement type: PIPE Subscription Agreements for preferred shares and warrants
  • Counterparty: Anchor PIPE Investor and other accredited investors
  • Signed / Effective: Jan 21 2026 / same
  • Duration / Termination: At closing
  • Reason: Provide committed financing to support the business combination

Original SEC Filing: Spring Valley Acquisition Corp. III [ SVAC ] - 8-K - Jan. 23, 2026

data.title: QHSLab Signs Note Repurchase Agreement With MedScience Research Group
data.description:
QHSLab entered into a Note Repurchase Agreement to retire outstanding debt held by MedScience Research Group. The company repurchased and cancelled a promissory note with $470,529 in principal and accrued interest as of December 31, 2025, issuing 1,568,432 shares of common stock as consideration. Upon issuance, all obligations under the note were fully extinguished. Management expects the transaction to simplify the capital structure and remove a related-party liability.

Agreement details:

  • Agreement type: Note Repurchase Agreement to cancel promissory note
  • Counterparty: MedScience Research Group
  • Signed / Effective: Dec 31 2025 / same
  • Reason: Simplify capital structure and eliminate related-party debt

Original SEC Filing: QHSLab, Inc. [ USAQ ] - 8-K - Jan. 23, 2026

data.title: Pacific Oak Strategic Opportunity REIT Signs Multiple Material Agreements
data.description:
Pacific Oak Strategic Opportunity REIT announced a series of agreements to transition management and support its planned liquidation. The Company reached an intercompany support arrangement with its BVI subsidiary, which includes up to $905,000 of short-term funding and a shift to third-party service providers. The BVI engaged Westdale Asset Management to oversee asset operations and sales and hired R2 Advisors for accounting and reporting support, while the Company separately engaged R2 for corporate accounting and compliance. In connection with these moves, the Company will terminate its Advisory Agreement with Pacific Oak Capital Advisors effective January 31, 2026, with a multi-week transition period. The steps are intended to stabilize operations, maintain compliance, and facilitate asset dispositions.

Agreement 1: Pacific Oak Strategic Opportunity REIT Signs Support Agreement With Pacific Oak SOR (BVI) Holdings

  • Agreement type: Intercompany support and transition agreement with short-term funding
  • Counterparty: Pacific Oak SOR (BVI) Holdings
  • Signed / Effective: Jan 23 2026 / Jan 23 2026
  • Duration / Termination: At will
  • Reason: Support liquidation planning and transition to new managers

Agreement 2: Pacific Oak Strategic Opportunity REIT Signs Asset Management Agreement With Westdale Asset Management

  • Agreement type: Asset management agreement
  • Counterparty: Westdale Asset Management
  • Signed / Effective: Jan 22 2026 / Jan 22 2026
  • Duration / Termination: 1 year, auto-renewal
  • Reason: Professionalize oversight and execute asset dispositions

Agreement 3: Pacific Oak Strategic Opportunity REIT Signs Management Services Agreement With R2 Advisors

  • Agreement type: Management services agreement (BVI)
  • Counterparty: R2 Advisors
  • Signed / Effective: Jan 23 2026 / Jan 23 2026
  • Duration / Termination: 12 months
  • Reason: Ensure accounting and reporting continuity during transition

Agreement 4: Pacific Oak Strategic Opportunity REIT Signs Management Services Agreement With R2 Advisors

  • Agreement type: Management services agreement (Company)
  • Counterparty: R2 Advisors
  • Signed / Effective: Jan 23 2026 / Jan 23 2026
  • Duration / Termination: Through Aug 01 2026
  • Reason: Maintain compliance and reporting during liquidation planning

Agreement 5: Pacific Oak Strategic Opportunity REIT Terminates Advisory Agreement With Pacific Oak Capital Advisors

  • Agreement terminated: Advisory agreement
  • Counterparty: Pacific Oak Capital Advisors
  • Original agreement date: N/A
  • Termination date: Jan 31 2026
  • Termination type: Early
  • Exit fees / payments: None
  • Reason: Transition to new asset and accounting managers amid liquidation plan

Original SEC Filing: Pacific Oak Strategic Opportunity REIT, Inc. [ PCOK ] - 8-K - Jan. 23, 2026
